Container.Name: pia-tun

Branch.TagDescription: Latest stable release (multi-arch: amd64, arm64, armv7)

Container.Overview:
Feature-rich, zero-leak VPN gateway for Private Internet Access (PIA) over WireGuard. Run it as a network gateway and route other containers (qBittorrent, Deluge, Transmission, ...) through the tunnel so their traffic only ever leaves via PIA, protected by a strict killswitch.
Features: - Strict killswitch with fast engagement (zero-leak design) - WireGuard speed (greater than 95% of line speed) with automatic MSS clamping - Reliable reconnect that handles ISP outages gracefully - PIA port forwarding with auto keepalive/refresh, synced to qBittorrent / Deluge / Transmission (or a custom script) - Optional SOCKS5 + HTTP proxies so other machines can use the VPN - Encrypted DNS (DoT) support - Observability: /health, /ready, /metrics (Prometheus + JSON) - Smart lowest-latency server selection, dedicated IP support, multi-arch (amd64/arm64/armv7) - Auth token acquired automatically and kept fresh - no manual token needed
Setup: 1. Set PIA_USER and PIA_PASS (your Private Internet Access account). 2. Set LOCAL_NETWORKS to your LAN CIDR (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24) so you keep LAN access to this host and its containers. 3. (Optional) Pick PIA_LOCATIONS, e.g. ca_ontario,ca_toronto - leave 'all' to auto-pick the lowest-latency server.
Routing another container through the VPN (gateway pattern): - On the downstream container (e.g. qBittorrent), set Network Type to 'Container' and pick 'pia-tun' under Container Network (equivalent to --network=container:pia-tun). - Remove that container's own port mappings and instead publish those ports HERE, on pia-tun (add them as Port mappings on this template - a qBittorrent WebUI 8080 example is included). - Start pia-tun first; start the downstream container after.
Note: NET_ADMIN is granted via Extra Parameters (--cap-drop=ALL --cap-add=NET_ADMIN). Unraid has no WireGuard kernel module loaded for containers by default, so pia-tun automatically falls back to the userspace backend (wireguard-go) - no extra host configuration required. Not affiliated with Private Internet Access.
Container.Description:
Feature-rich and reliable VPN container for PIA + WireGuard, designed to act as a network gateway for other containers.
PIA_USER and PIA_PASS are the only required values. Everything else is optional and most defaults are fine for typical use. See the full variable reference at https://github.com/x0lie/pia-tun/blob/main/docs/env.md and copy-paste compose examples at https://github.com/x0lie/pia-tun/tree/main/docs/compose-examples.
The qBittorrent WebUI port (8080) below is included as an example of the gateway pattern: it is published on pia-tun so a qBittorrent container started with --network=container:pia-tun is reachable from your LAN while its traffic flows through the tunnel.

Config.Name: PIA_USER
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Your Private Internet Access username (typically starts with p).

Config.Name: PIA_PASS
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Your Private Internet Access password.

Config.Name: PIA_LOCATIONS
Config.Description: Comma-separated PIA region IDs (e.g. ca_ontario,ca_toronto). pia-tun tests latency and connects to the best one. Leave 'all' to consider every region. Region IDs: https://serverlist.piaservers.net/vpninfo/servers/v6

Config.Name: LOCAL_NETWORKS
Config.Description: CIDR range(s) allowed to bypass the tunnel so you keep LAN access to this host and its containers (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24). Comma-separated, IPv4/IPv6. Use 'all' for all private ranges, 'none' to disable. The attached container network is auto-included unless set to 'none'.

Config.Name: qBittorrent WebUI (example)
Config.Description: Example downstream port: publish here the WebUI/ports of any container you route through pia-tun with --network=container:pia-tun (here, qBittorrent's 8080). Remove or change if you are not running qBittorrent behind the VPN.

Config.Name: PS_CLIENT
Config.Description: Auto-sync the forwarded port to a torrent client: qbittorrent, transmission, or deluge. Enables PIA port forwarding automatically. Leave empty if unused (use PS_SCRIPT for unsupported clients).

Config.Name: PS_USER
Config.Description: Username for the PS_CLIENT API. For qBittorrent you can instead enable 'Bypass authentication for clients on localhost' and leave this empty.

Config.Name: PS_PASS
Config.Description: Password for the PS_CLIENT API (see PS_USER note).

Config.Name: PS_URL
Config.Description: PS_CLIENT API endpoint. Auto-set to http://localhost:{8080 qbittorrent | 9091 transmission | 8112 deluge}. Override only if the client listens on a non-default port, e.g. http://localhost:8081

Config.Name: PS_SCRIPT
Config.Description: Path to a custom script run after each port refresh (for clients PS_CLIENT does not support). See https://github.com/x0lie/pia-tun/blob/main/docs/compose-examples/ps-script.md

Config.Name: PF_ENABLED
Config.Description: Enable PIA port forwarding manually. Automatically enabled when PS_CLIENT or PS_SCRIPT is set.

Config.Name: PIA_DIP_TOKEN
Config.Description: PIA Dedicated IP token to connect to your reserved PIA IP. Leave empty for standard servers.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Timezone for log timestamps, e.g. America/New_York or Europe/Paris. Defaults to UTC.

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Logging verbosity: error, info, debug, trace.

Config.Name: DNS
Config.Description: DNS resolver: 'pia', 'system', DoT (e.g. tls://one.one.one.one,dns.mullvad.net), or Do53 (e.g. 1.1.1.1,8.8.8.8). Avoid 'system' unless you understand your host resolver (DNS leak risk).

Config.Name: BOOTSTRAP_DNS
Config.Description: Do53-only resolver used to bootstrap PIA hostnames. Leave empty for the default (149.112.112.9, 149.112.112.11). Must not overlap with DNS.

Config.Name: MTU
Config.Description: Max packet size for the WireGuard interface (pia0). Lower it only if you have MTU issues on your link.

Config.Name: WG_BACKEND
Config.Description: WireGuard implementation: 'kernel' (faster) or 'userspace' (wireguard-go). Auto-detected if empty; on Unraid this is typically userspace.

Config.Name: IPT_BACKEND
Config.Description: iptables backend: 'nft' or 'legacy'. Auto-detected if empty.

Config.Name: SOCKS5_ENABLED
Config.Description: Enable the SOCKS5 proxy so other machines/containers can use the VPN. If enabled, also add a Port mapping for SOCKS5_PORT (1080).

Config.Name: SOCKS5 Port
Config.Description: SOCKS5 proxy listen port. Only relevant when SOCKS5_ENABLED=true.

Config.Name: HTTP_PROXY_ENABLED
Config.Description: Enable the HTTP proxy so other machines/containers can use the VPN. If enabled, also add a Port mapping for HTTP_PROXY_PORT (8888).

Config.Name: HTTP Proxy Port
Config.Description: HTTP proxy listen port. Only relevant when HTTP_PROXY_ENABLED=true.

Config.Name: PROXY_USER
Config.Description: Optional username to require authentication on the SOCKS5/HTTP proxies.

Config.Name: PROXY_PASS
Config.Description: Optional password for proxy authentication.

Config.Name: METRICS_ENABLED
Config.Description: Serve /health, /ready, /metrics and /metrics?format=json. Disable to turn off the metrics server entirely.

Config.Name: Metrics Port
Config.Description: Port serving /health, /ready and /metrics (Prometheus). Only needed if you scrape metrics or want to reach the health endpoints from outside.

Config.Name: METRICS_PORT
Config.Description: Internal port for the metrics/health server. Only change if you also change the Metrics Port mapping above.

Config.Name: INSTANCE_NAME
Config.Description: Prometheus label to distinguish multiple pia-tun containers. Leave empty if you run only one.

Config.Name: HC_INTERVAL
Config.Description: Health check frequency in seconds (probes 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, 9.9.9.9 through the tunnel). Default is fine for most.

Config.Name: HC_FAILURE_WINDOW
Config.Description: Seconds of continuously failing health checks before triggering a reconnect. Increase if your ISP drops briefly and triggers reconnects too eagerly.

Config.Name: GET_REAL_IP
Config.Description: Fetch and log your pre-VPN public IP on startup (useful to confirm the tunnel changed it). Set false for a minimal-egress setup.
